entities.name: Moshi Moshi Retail Corporation Public Company Limited

financial.note: KBANK (via Kasikorn Securities) acts as a primary advisor and liquidity provider; while MOSHI maintains a net cash position in 2026, expansion credit lines are sensitive to the 1.00% BoT policy rate.
Provides trade finance facilities for cross-border sourcing; interest expenses on short-term working capital are sensitive to prime lending rate fluctuations.

supply_chain.note: Approximately 50-60% of lifestyle products are exclusively designed and imported from China; margins are highly sensitive to Chinese production costs and labor availability.
50/50 JV for event-based marketing (e.g., Sanrio exhibitions) designed to drive brand engagement and higher-margin licensed product sales.
Primary expansion nodes; MOSHI's revenue growth is directly linked to foot traffic in hypermarket corridors and favorable rental lease renewals.

competitors.note: Primary global rival; 2026 data shows MOSHI gaining share as Miniso recalibrates its high-cost mall strategy in Thailand.
Intense competition in the large-format lifestyle segment; KKV's 2026 downsizing provides a consolidation opportunity for MOSHI's standalone stores.
Traditional competitor in the fixed-price segment; MOSHI competes through localized designs and broader price tiers.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Major impact on COGS for imports; a strong Yuan (CNY) increases procurement costs and compresses gross profit margins (GPM).
Conflict-driven 30-40% spike in Q1 2026 resin prices increases manufacturing costs for household goods and toys, affecting 20% of COGS.
MOSHI products are discretionary; higher confidence correlates with impulse purchase conversion, a key 2026 management strategy.
Rising shipping costs from Chinese ports to Laem Chabang directly increase landing costs for high-volume, low-margin SKUs.
